zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Thomas Robert Morton Lee於1458年5月8日出生在英國史洛普爾時，他的父親Thomas de Moreton47歲，母親Lady Juliana Boteler43歲。他於1484年與Elizabeth Anne Ashley在英國英格蘭史洛普爾結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。他去世於1526年2月6日在英國坎布利其爾可托享年67歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Thomas Robert Morton Lee于1458年5月8日出生在英国史洛普爾时，他的父亲Thomas de Moreton47岁，母亲Lady Juliana Boteler43岁。他于1484年与Elizabeth Anne Ashley在英国英格兰史洛普爾结婚。他们至少是2个女儿的父母。他去世于1526年2月6日在英国坎布利其爾可托享年67岁。

nameMeanings.name: Morton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of the many places called Mor(e)ton, named with Old English mōr ‘moor’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. There has probably been some confusion with Morten , and perhaps also with Murton . This English name has also been established in Ireland since the 13th century.
American shortened and altered form of Swedish Mårtensson or Mortensson (see Martenson and Mortenson ), which could also be substituted for the Finnish cognate Marttinen.
French: shortened form of   Moreton   3.

variants.name: Moreton
meanings: English (West Midlands): variant of   Morton   1.
Spanish (Moretón): nickname from moretón ‘brown, tanned’ (of skin).
French: nickname from a double diminutive of   More   2 ‘Moor’. Compare   Moret  .

variants.name: Murton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places in northern England, all named in Old English as ‘settlement or enclosure at the marsh or moor’, from mōr ‘marsh, fen, moor’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’. There has been considerable confusion with both Morton and Merton .

variants.name: Morten
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Mortagne in La Manche, France. This surname has probably been confused with   Morton  .

nameMeanings.name: Lee
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sang, Jae, Jong, Jung, Sung, Yong, Kyung, Seung, Dong, Kwang, Myung.

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a meadow or a patch of arable land, Middle English lee, lea, from Old English lēa, dative case (used after a preposition) of lēah, which originally meant ‘wood or glade’.

English: habitational name from any of the many places in England named with Old English lēah ‘wood, glade’, including Lee in Buckinghamshire, Essex, Hampshire, Kent, and Shropshire, and Lea in Cheshire, Derbyshire, Herefordshire, Lancashire, Lincolnshire, and Wiltshire.

variants.name: Ley
meanings: English: variant of   Lee  .
German: from a medieval personal name, Eloy, from Latin Eligius (see Eligio ), a Christian saint's name.
German (Rhineland): topographic name from Middle High German leie ‘rock, stone, slate’, or a habitational name from any of several places called with this word. Compare Leier .

variants.name: Lees
meanings: English and Scottish: topographic name from Middle English lees ‘fields, arable land’, plural of lee (see Lee ), or from Middle English lese ‘pasture, meadow’ (Old English lǣs).

English: habitational name fromLeece or Lees in Lancashire, or Leese in Cheshire, all named from OldEnglish lēas ‘woodland clearings’ (plural oflēah), or from Leece in Cumbria, which was probably namedwith a Celtic word, lïss ‘hall, court, the principalhouse in a district’.
English: variant of   Leece   1.

variants.name: Lay
meanings: English: variant of   Lee  .
Scottish: shortened form of   McClay  .
German: habitational name from any of the places so named, in the Rhineland near Koblenz and in Bavaria, from lay(h), a word meaning ‘stone, rock, slate’.

variants.name: Laye
meanings: English: variant of   Lee  .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 李, based on its Cantonese pronunciation. See   Li   1.

variants.name: Lie
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Oystein, Sven, Thor.  German Hans, Gunther, Hilde.  Korean Han, Chang Ho, Chen, Chun, Hok, Jung, Kam, Liang, Lie, Ling, Shuen, Yung.
Norwegian: variant of Li 11, a habitational name from any of numerous farmsteads named Li, from Old Norse hlíth ‘mountain slope, hillside’. In Norway this is the most frequent spelling.
German: unexplained.

variants.name: Layman
meanings: English: topographic name for a dweller by a woodland clearing or a meadow, from Middle English ley + man. Compare Lee .
English: nickname from layman, a word denoting an uneducated person.
Americanized form of German   Lehmann  .

variants.name: Lea
meanings: English: variant of   Lee  .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 李 and 利, see   Li   1 and 3.

variants.name: Lye
meanings: English: variant of   Lee   1, a topographic name for someone who lived near a patch of arable land, or a habitational name from Lye in Herefordshire (with the same etymology).

Norwegian: habitational name from a farmstead in Rogaland named Lye, Old Norse Lýgi meaning ‘alliance, covenant’, used to denote a place sanctified by such an agreement, such as a court or council meeting place.
Altered form of German   Ley  .

variants.name: Saelee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Chan, Kao, Nai, Chai, Cheng, Chien, Foo, Hin, Muang, Pao, Seng, Song, Sou.
Southeast Asian (Iu Mien): from the name of the Lee clan of the Iu Mien people from Laos + the Thai prefix sae, denoting a clan. Compare Lee 8.

variants.name: Leigh
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places (in at least sixteen counties, but especially Leigh in Lancashire) named either with the nominative case of Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’ (see Lee ) or with lēage, a late dative form of this word (see Lye ).
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 李, based on its Cantonese pronunciation. See   Li   1.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Thomas Robert Morton Lee was born on 8 May 1458, in Shropshire, England, his father, Thomas de Moreton, was 47 and his mother, Lady Juliana Boteler, was 43. He married Elizabeth Anne Ashley in 1484, in Shropshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. He died on 6 February 1526, in Coton, Cambridgeshire, England, at the age of 67.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Thomas Robert Morton Lee est né le 8 mai 1458, à Shropshire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas de Moreton, avait 47 ans et sa mère, Lady Juliana Boteler, avait 43 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Anne Ashley en 1484, à Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Il est décédé le 6 février 1526, à Coton, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 67 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Thomas Robert Morton Lee am 8. Mai 1458 in Shropshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas de Moreton 47 und seine Mutter Lady Juliana Boteler 43. Er hat 1484 in Shropshire, England, United Kingdom Elizabeth Anne Ashley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Töchter. Er starb am 6. Februar 1526 im Alter von 67 Jahren in Coton, Cambridgeshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Thomas Robert Morton Lee è nato il il 8 maggio 1458 nel Shropshire, England, il suo padre, Thomas de Moreton, aveva 47 anni e il suo madre, Lady Juliana Boteler, aveva 43 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Anne Ashley nel 1484, nel Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figlie. Morì il 6 febbraio 1526, nel Coton, Cambridgeshire, England, all'età di 67 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas Robert Morton Lee(이)가 1458년 5월 8일 영국 슈럽실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas de Moreton(은)는 47세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Juliana Boteler(은)는 43세였다. 그는 1484년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르에서 Elizabeth Anne Ashley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1526년 2월 6일 영국 캄브리지실르 컷에서 67세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Anne Ashley em 1484, em Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhas. Он умер 6 февраля 1526 г. в местности котон, камбриджешир, Англия в возрасте 67 лет.
